{{Quote|Looks like it was built for children.|[[Tahiri Veila]]|<ref name="Rebirth">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory II: Rebirth]]''</ref>}}
'''Yag-prime''' was an [[Empress-class space station|''Empress''-class space station]] orbiting the [[Givin/Legends|Givin]] homeworld [[Yag'Dhul/Legends|Yag'Dhul]]. It played a crucial role in many battles, including the [[Bacta War]] and the [[Battle of Yag'Dhul (26 ABY)|Battle of Yag'Dhul]].

==Construction==
The station had a central disk with thick expanses of living quarters above and below. Multiple thin towers rose from the middle of the disk, which made the station look like it had been impaled on spears. Three wedge-shaped causeways for launch-and-recovery purposes stabbed out into space from the central disk.<ref name="Krytos" />

Due to construction costs space was a very premium commodity. [[Refresher/Legends|Refresher]] stations and dining facilities were communal. While there were private rooms for dinner meetings, all of the food was prepared in a central galley and delivered to the half-dozen dining facilities on the base. Those same rooms also served as lounges and recreation facilities. The internal corridors were strikingly small, designed to make it easier to defend against a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] assault.<ref name="Bacta">''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]''</ref> It is not known if this feature was common to all ''Empress''-class stations, or if the design was also constructed by groups outside the Rebellion.

There were twenty-five living levels above the docking facility and twenty-five below. The main docking area contained ten levels with the middle six dealing with cargo transfer and storage while the outer two on each side contain crew housing, small shops and the [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcafes]] [[Hyperspace (tapcaf)|Hyperspace]] and [[Flarestar]].<ref name="Bacta" />

==History==
===Warlord Zsinj===
{{Quote|The bacta is being held here, and we're going to get it away from them.|Wedge Antilles to Rogue Squadron|<ref name="Krytos" />}}
By [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], however, Yag-prime had been seized by the forces of [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]], although the running of the station was in the hands of a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] merchant named [[Valsil Torr]]. Zsinj used the station to store a large quantity of [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] which had been seized from a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] convoy, but the New Republic was notified by [[Qlaern Hirf]] of the [[Ashern]]. [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] and [[Defender Wing]] soon arrived with a group of assault shuttles and [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]], with which they took control of the station and recovered the bacta after a short [[Raid on Yag-prime|battle]].<ref name="Krytos" /> The damage to the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] was repaired by [[Givin/Legends|Givin]], as they could survive in vacuum.

===The Bacta War===
{{Quote|Interesting place you have here. Not enough to stop a Super Star Destroyer, but you know that. Still, if you have to die in a box in space, this looks as good as any in which to do it.|Booster Terrik to Wedge Antilles|<ref name="Bacta" />}}
Yag-prime played a prominent role in the [[Bacta War]]. After resigning their commissions, Wedge Antilles and the rest of Rogue Squadron were looking for an appropriate base of operations in their fight against [[Ysanne Isard]]. [[Pash Cracken]], a former member of the squadron, was tasked with rendering the station uninhabitable in order to prevent Zsinj from using it again. Instead, he falsely reported the station as destroyed to his superiors. Shortly after, the Rogues moved in.<ref name="Bacta" />

====Under Booster Terrik====
{{Quote|Booster Terrik, this station is all yours. May the Force be with you.|Wedge Antilles|<ref name="Bacta" />}}
After the ''[[Pulsar Skate]]'' returned after its mission to recover the remaining [[Eidolon (Strike-class)|''Eidolon'']] materials on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], they brought along [[Booster Terrik]], to the delight of Antilles. He then offered to act as the station manager due to the promise of it not being boring and being pitted against [[Fliry Vorru]] in a war of trade and information.<ref name="Bacta" />

Terrik made many changes to the station, such as lowering the station temperature by an average of five degrees. He refused to heat unoccupied portions of the station, thereby lowering its operating costs. By leaving the tapcafs and stores warmer to encourage the people to congregate and patronize those establishments, he turned a profit, since the station's vendors were paying him a percentage of their own profits and were funneling all their supply needs through him.<ref name="Bacta" />

Shortly after advising Antilles of the folly of closing the station to outsiders, Terrik reopened the station to trade in order to generate capital for Antilles' operation. His plan was to have people bring them information and equipment instead of having them go out and get it, and to develop suppliers who were in their debt because of the station. By doing this, he discouraged the people who frequented the station from betraying them to Isard.<ref name="Bacta" />

In accordance with the secret plans made with Wedge Antilles and [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]], Booster Terrik made many modifications to the station. He purchased many [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]], [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] and targeting sensors from [[Talon Karrde]]. The sensors were fitted into the station, while the torpedoes and missiles were fitted into a series of [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] ranging in size and including a Corellian Light Freighter.<ref name="Bacta" />

====Defying ''Lusankya''====
{{Quote|I'll give you those five minutes before I destroy your ship.|Booster Terrik to [[Joak Drysso]]|<ref name="Bacta" />}}
Shortly before the decisive [[Battle of Thyferra]], Isard's spy [[Melina Carniss]] was allowed to learn that the Rogues were based on Yag-prime. As Antilles intended, Isard sent her [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'' and [[Errant Venture/Legends|''Virulence'']] to destroy the station. When Isard's ships arrived, Antilles' small fleet jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] toward [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]]. Terrik then caught ''Lusankya'' in Yag-prime's [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]] and activated the [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]], pinning it in place despite its attempts to break free by setting the engines to full reverse. After his offer of surrender was rejected by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Joak Drysso]], Terrik activated the torpedo targeting sensors, gaining over three hundred locks upon ''Lusankya'', enough to destroy the huge ship.<ref name="Bacta" />

In reality, though, it was an elaborate bluff orchestrated by Antilles. The station had no proton torpedoes, which were all aboard the small fleet of freighters that jumped with Antilles towards Thyferra. All the station had were the targeting locks.<ref name="NewEssential">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

At this point Captain [[Lakwii Varrscha]] of ''Virulence'' quickly intervened, moving her ship into the path of the tractor beams and allowing ''Lusankya'' to escape, abandoning its [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] escort, which were then destroyed when Pash Cracken's [[Ace Squadron (New Republic)|Ace Squadron]] was pulled out of hyperspace by the gravity well projector. Believing she had been caught in a New Republic trap, Varrscha surrendered to Terrik, who promptly took command of ''Virulence'' and took it to Thyferra, where it would play a crucial role in the [[Battle of Thyferra|battle]].<ref name="Bacta" />

Shortly after the [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|Bastion Accords]] were signed in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], the station was ceded to the Givin, who used it to defend the Yag'Dhul shipyard complex, and stocked it with a unit of their own swift starfighters.<ref name="Rebirth" />

===Yuuzhan Vong War===
During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], Yag-prime was commanded by [[Dodecian]] [[Illiet]]. It was on Yag'Dhul Station that the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Kelbis Nu]] realized [[Nom Anor]]'s true identity. He managed to escape the station but was killed by agents of the [[Peace Brigade]] on [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]]. Shortly after, [[Anakin Solo]], [[Tahiri Veila]] and [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] arrived on the station aboard a commandeered Yuuzhan Vong [[Stalking Moon|scout ship]], with a warning of impending invasion from the Yuuzhan Vong. They were interrogated by Dodecian Illiet and after a few hours, were set free when their warning of an invasion was proven true.<ref name="Rebirth" />

While on their way to the storage lockers of [[Ring 1-C]] to obtain [[Environment suit/Legends|vac suits]], they were attacked by Yuuzhan Vong [[Warrior caste|warriors]] and Nom Anor, who was wearing a [[ooglith masquer]] to pose as a Givin. A duel ensued between Anakin and [[Shok Choka]] when Anor declined Corran's challenge. Anakin was able to defeat Choka and the three escaped and managed to reach [[Docking Port 12 Berth 13]] where a ship was waiting for them.<ref name="Rebirth" />

It is not known whether the station survived the [[Battle of Yag'Dhul (26 ABY)|Battle of Yag'Dhul]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Givin decorative motifs, I'd guess. Rogue Squadron sure didn't paint this stuff.|[[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] regarding the wall designs|<ref name="Rebirth" />}}
The station was a trade staple in the region for a long time, according to Booster Terrik.<ref name="Bacta" /> It was very compact to an average [[Human/Legends|human]] and delicate, with the danger of suffering explosive decompression. Its walls were covered with fractal patterns and notation in some sort of script, presumably written by the Givin.<ref name="Rebirth" />

==Behind the scenes==
Yag-prime, as it is described in ''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]'' and depicted on the cover of the book, bears striking similarity to the space platforms in the video game [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], and may have been based on such stations.
